payload_json: {"performance_statement": "Assess a practical claim, cite the supporting evidence, and explain whether the claim is usable in a real decision.", "inputs": ["claim or recommendation", "source list", "publication dates", "local constraints"], "tools": ["web browser", "notebook or worksheet", "AI assistant (optional)"], "materials": ["claim-check worksheet"], "steps_summary": ["State the claim in one sentence.", "Identify the original source, date, and evidence offered.", "Compare at least two independent sources or one primary source and one reviewer.", "Mark what is known, unknown, and locally variable.", "Record a decision with a citation trail."], "success_criteria": ["The worksheet names the claim, source, date, and evidence quality.", "The learner can distinguish primary from secondary support.", "The final note states whether to act, wait, test, or reject."], "failure_modes": ["Treating popularity as proof.", "Ignoring dates and using outdated guidance.", "Failing to separate general advice from local conditions."], "safety_boundary": "This skill does not authorize high-risk action; when consequences are significant, escalate to qualified local guidance and primary sources."}

{"definition": "A shared language of quantity that allows materials, loads, water, time, and space to be compared without confusion.", "key_questions": ["What quantity am I measuring?", "What unit is appropriate for this decision?", "How do I convert between common household and technical units?"], "misconceptions": ["Precision and accuracy are the same thing.", "Bigger numbers always mean more capacity regardless of unit.", "Approximation is useless unless it is exact."], "formulas_or_rules": ["Choose one working unit before calculating.", "Write units next to every number.", "Estimate first, then calculate, then sense-check."], "units": ["gallon", "liter", "watt", "watt-hour", "volt", "amp", "square foot", "degree F", "degree C", "psi"]}

{"performance_statement": "Create a clear map of a household or local system that shows how resources flow, where bottlenecks exist, and what fails first.", "inputs": ["target system", "basic measurements", "known dependencies", "failure scenarios"], "tools": ["paper or whiteboard", "markers", "camera or scanner (optional)"], "materials": ["system mapping template"], "steps_summary": ["Choose a system boundary such as water, food, shelter, or power.", "List inputs, outputs, storage points, dependencies, and single points of failure.", "Mark critical nodes, backup paths, and failure consequences.", "Rank which dependencies are easy, medium, or hard to replace.", "Convert the map into a one-page operating view."], "success_criteria": ["The map shows inputs, outputs, and at least one likely failure path.", "Critical dependencies are visible and ranked.", "Another person can read the map and understand the system."], "failure_modes": ["Drawing a process list instead of a system map.", "Ignoring hidden dependencies such as fuel, internet, or weather.", "Making the diagram so detailed that it cannot guide action."], "safety_boundary": "This activity is observational and planning-oriented; do not test failure scenarios on live systems unless it is safe and reversible."}

{"performance_statement": "Use AI to speed up explanation and comparison while preserving a verifiable chain back to sources, dates, and local constraints.", "inputs": ["question or task", "source material", "local context such as budget or climate"], "tools": ["AI assistant", "browser", "note template"], "materials": ["prompt pack", "claim-check worksheet"], "steps_summary": ["Frame the task in plain language and define the decision to be made.", "Ask AI to summarize and compare sources with explicit citations or references.", "Cross-check the high-risk or time-sensitive claims against primary or recent sources.", "Ask AI to localize the plan by budget, region, housing type, or available tools.", "Save the final decision note with sources and caveats."], "success_criteria": ["The output names which claims were source-checked.", "The learner can explain where AI helped and where human verification took over.", "The final note is adapted to a real local scenario."], "failure_modes": ["Accepting confident wording as evidence.", "Prompting for conclusions without defining constraints.", "Forgetting to verify recency, law, code, or safety boundaries."], "safety_boundary": "AI outputs are draft material; do not use them as sole authority for code, medicine, legal matters, or high-risk technical action."}

{"goal": "Produce a single reference packet that shows what the household needs to stay functional for 72 hours and two weeks.", "deliverables": ["household inventory sheet", "critical-needs list", "contacts list", "priority gap list"], "acceptance_criteria": ["The inventory covers water, food, shelter, sanitation, communications, medicines, transport, and power.", "Every item has a quantity, location, and owner or steward where relevant.", "The packet highlights the top five vulnerabilities and the easiest next improvements."], "time_box_hours": 3.0, "budget_band": "low", "team_size": 1}

{"performance_statement": "Calculate water quantities for a time window, choose appropriate containers, and label and rotate stored water correctly.", "inputs": ["household size", "storage duration", "container options", "space constraints"], "tools": ["marker", "measuring container", "storage worksheet"], "materials": ["food-safe containers", "labels", "cleaning supplies"], "steps_summary": ["Estimate minimum and preferred daily water quantities per person and use case.", "Match the total volume to actual containers and storage locations.", "Label date, source, intended use, and rotation interval.", "Separate drinking water from utility water if using both.", "Record the plan in a storage table."], "success_criteria": ["The plan covers at least one minimum-duration target.", "Containers are identified, dated, and assigned to uses.", "Storage locations are realistic for the dwelling."], "failure_modes": ["Using unknown or contaminated containers.", "Storing too little water for heat, pets, or sanitation needs.", "Failing to label and rotate containers."], "safety_boundary": "Use only appropriate containers and local health guidance; storage does not replace proper treatment when source quality is uncertain."}

{"definition": "A decision framework for matching likely water hazards to suitable treatment methods and limits.", "key_questions": ["What problem am I treating: particles, microbes, chemicals, or salinity?", "What does this treatment remove well, poorly, or not at all?", "What are the capacity, maintenance, and failure signs of the method?"], "misconceptions": ["All filters remove all contaminants.", "Clear water is necessarily safe water.", "Boiling solves chemical contamination."], "formulas_or_rules": ["Match method to contaminant class.", "Multiple barriers are often stronger than one.", "Capacity, contact time, and maintenance matter as much as device choice."], "units": ["microns", "liters", "gallons", "minutes", "ppm"]}

{"performance_statement": "Perform a basic set of observable and kit-based checks, document what they mean, and decide whether to store, retreat, or reject the water.", "inputs": ["water sample", "test method or kit", "decision thresholds", "log sheet"], "tools": ["test kit or strips", "clean sample container", "timer", "pen"], "materials": ["log sheet"], "steps_summary": ["Inspect the sample for clarity, odor, container condition, and labeling.", "Run the selected household-level test according to its instructions.", "Record date, source, test type, result, and interpretation.", "Compare against the chosen threshold or action rule.", "Decide whether to store, retreat, re-test, or reject."], "success_criteria": ["The log captures the source, date, method, and decision.", "The learner can explain the limit of the chosen test.", "The final action is consistent with the result and context."], "failure_modes": ["Using expired or misread test materials.", "Drawing conclusions beyond what the kit can show.", "Failing to document the sample source and date."], "safety_boundary": "Household tests are screening tools; if the consequences of error are high, use certified testing and local public-health guidance."}

{"goal": "Build a household-ready plan that covers how much water is needed, where it comes from, how it is treated, how it is tested, and what to do when one path fails.", "deliverables": ["household water plan", "source and storage map", "treatment decision matrix", "retest schedule"], "acceptance_criteria": ["The plan covers drinking, hygiene, food prep, pets, and essential cleaning where relevant.", "At least one backup source or fallback path is named.", "The plan can be followed by another household member without additional explanation."], "time_box_hours": 3.0, "budget_band": "low", "team_size": 1}

{"goal": "Create a pantry layout and stocking plan that matches the household's real diet, budget, storage space, and disruption window.", "deliverables": ["pantry map", "category labels", "shopping gap list", "minimum-duration stock target"], "acceptance_criteria": ["The design covers staple foods, proteins, fats, seasonings, and special dietary needs where applicable.", "Storage conditions and package sizes fit the dwelling and cooking setup.", "The output distinguishes minimum stock from aspirational stock."], "time_box_hours": 3.0, "budget_band": "low", "team_size": 1}

{"performance_statement": "Use labels, placement rules, and a simple review schedule to keep pantry stock fresh and aligned to actual use.", "inputs": ["pantry layout", "current stock", "date labels", "review interval"], "tools": ["marker", "labels", "shelf or bin system", "inventory sheet"], "materials": ["rotation sheet"], "steps_summary": ["Group items by use, shelf life, and replacement frequency.", "Place older stock in front and newer stock in back.", "Record quantities and trigger points for replenishment.", "Review the pantry on a repeating schedule.", "Retire, donate, or repurpose items before they become waste."], "success_criteria": ["The household can find and use stock without guesswork.", "Review dates and reorder triggers are visible.", "Expired or stale items are not accumulating unnoticed."], "failure_modes": ["Buying without a rotation habit.", "Storing items that no one regularly cooks or eats.", "Failing to set reorder triggers."], "safety_boundary": "Follow local food-safety guidance and product labeling; when in doubt about safety, discard rather than gamble."}

{"definition": "A conceptual boundary map showing which preservation methods fit which foods, what their limits are, and when guidance must come from authoritative process sources.", "key_questions": ["Which preservation methods are low-risk vs higher-risk?", "What environmental factors make preserved food safer or less safe?", "When is authoritative process guidance essential?"], "misconceptions": ["Any preserved food that looks fine is safe.", "A successful batch proves the method was sound.", "Drying, fermenting, freezing, and canning have the same risk profile."], "formulas_or_rules": ["Match method to food type and moisture/acid profile.", "Use authoritative process guidance for higher-risk methods.", "When a method depends on exact procedure, improvisation shrinks safety margin."], "units": ["time", "temperature", "pH", "water activity"]}

{"performance_statement": "Start seeds in a controlled way, document the variables, and produce healthy seedlings or a clear diagnosis of what failed.", "inputs": ["crop choice", "planting date", "light plan", "watering plan"], "tools": ["seed trays or containers", "labels", "light source or window", "timer (optional)"], "materials": ["seed mix", "seeds", "water", "log sheet"], "steps_summary": ["Choose crops and dates based on local season and transplant window.", "Prepare containers, medium, labels, and a light plan.", "Sow at the correct depth and moisture level.", "Track emergence, watering, and light exposure in a log.", "Harden off or re-seed based on results."], "success_criteria": ["Containers are labeled and dated.", "The log shows sowing date, crop, and emergence observations.", "The learner can explain at least one likely cause if germination or growth is weak."], "failure_modes": ["Starting too early or too late for local conditions.", "Under-lighting seedlings and producing weak growth.", "Overwatering and damping off."], "safety_boundary": "Follow seed packet and local climate guidance; avoid introducing contaminated soil or plants into indoor living spaces."}

{"goal": "Design a manageable growing system that matches the site, season, water access, labor, and learning goals.", "deliverables": ["garden layout", "crop plan", "care calendar", "material list"], "acceptance_criteria": ["The plan fits the actual sunlight, water access, and available time.", "The crop mix includes at least one reliable and one learning-oriented crop.", "The layout and calendar are usable without further interpretation."], "time_box_hours": 4.0, "budget_band": "low", "team_size": 1}

{"definition": "A simple model of how heat, air, and moisture move through walls, roofs, floors, windows, and gaps.", "key_questions": ["Where does heat escape or enter most easily?", "How do air leaks differ from insulation gaps?", "When does moisture turn from nuisance into building damage?"], "misconceptions": ["Insulation alone solves all comfort problems.", "Condensation is purely cosmetic.", "More sealing is always better even if ventilation is ignored."], "formulas_or_rules": ["Air sealing and insulation solve different but related problems.", "Warm air carries moisture; moisture management is part of durability.", "Control layers work best when they are continuous."], "units": ["degree F", "degree C", "R-value", "humidity", "square foot"]}

{"goal": "Inspect the building or dwelling for obvious air leakage, weak spots, and easy improvements, then rank them by impact and effort.", "deliverables": ["weatherization audit", "priority fix list", "room-by-room notes", "photo log"], "acceptance_criteria": ["The audit identifies where drafts, weak insulation, or leakage are likely.", "The output ranks fixes by impact, cost, and reversibility.", "The list clearly distinguishes owner-level from renter-friendly actions."], "time_box_hours": 3.0, "budget_band": "low", "team_size": 1}

{"performance_statement": "Identify likely moisture sources, document conditions that keep surfaces damp, and create a response plan that removes the source or shortens exposure.", "inputs": ["visible conditions", "humidity or dampness observations", "space usage patterns"], "tools": ["flashlight", "checklist", "camera or notebook"], "materials": ["moisture checklist"], "steps_summary": ["Inspect areas near plumbing, exterior walls, windows, bathrooms, kitchens, and basements or crawlspaces.", "Classify the moisture source: leak, condensation, infiltration, or occupant load.", "Record what keeps the area damp: poor airflow, low heat, trapped vapor, or repeated wetting.", "Choose corrective actions that target the source first.", "Set a follow-up check date."], "success_criteria": ["The response plan targets the source, not only the surface.", "Areas needing escalation are clearly identified.", "The learner can explain the difference between leak-driven and condensation-driven problems."], "failure_modes": ["Treating visible mold as only a cleaning problem.", "Ignoring intermittent leaks or seasonal condensation patterns.", "Failing to recheck after the first intervention."], "safety_boundary": "Escalate persistent, extensive, or health-sensitive cases to qualified local guidance; do not disturb hazardous materials or hidden cavities without appropriate precautions."}

{"performance_statement": "Demonstrate correct selection and basic use of essential hand tools for common household tasks.", "inputs": ["task list", "tool list", "fastener types", "material types"], "tools": ["measuring tape", "screwdrivers", "adjustable wrench", "pliers", "utility knife", "hammer"], "materials": ["sample fasteners or scrap material", "checklist"], "steps_summary": ["Match each tool to the task and material.", "Demonstrate measuring, fastening, loosening, cutting, and gripping with control.", "Identify common fastener types and the right driver or wrench fit.", "Store tools and blades safely after use.", "Record gaps in the starter tool list."], "success_criteria": ["The learner uses tools without damaging the fastener or material.", "The learner can name when not to force a tool fit.", "The checklist shows which core tools are present or still needed."], "failure_modes": ["Using the wrong size driver or wrench.", "Applying force before understanding the fastener direction or material.", "Neglecting blade safety and hand placement."], "safety_boundary": "Do not improvise on energized, pressurized, or structurally critical systems when the task exceeds basic hand-tool work."}

{"performance_statement": "Find the key shutoffs, assemble a small response kit, and apply simple reversible fixes for gaps, drips, and minor surface damage.", "inputs": ["household layout", "known utility fixtures", "minor repair materials"], "tools": ["flashlight", "hand tools", "marker", "camera"], "materials": ["sealants or weatherstripping", "absorbent materials", "temporary patch supplies", "checklist"], "steps_summary": ["Locate water and utility shutoffs and label or map them.", "Assemble a small kit for leaks, drafts, and minor patching.", "Apply simple sealing or temporary patching to a low-risk target.", "Document what worked, what did not, and what still needs escalation.", "Store the map and kit where others can find them."], "success_criteria": ["Shutoff locations are recorded and usable under stress.", "The patch or seal improves the problem without creating a larger one.", "The learner can tell temporary repair from durable repair."], "failure_modes": ["Not knowing the right shutoff until an emergency starts.", "Using the wrong sealant or patch for the material or moisture condition.", "Treating a temporary fix as a permanent solution."], "safety_boundary": "Temporary repairs buy time; stop and escalate when structural, electrical, gas, or concealed damage is possible."}

{"definition": "A beginner model of household electrical loads, circuit limits, and the relationship between power and stored energy.", "key_questions": ["What is the difference between watts and watt-hours?", "Why do some devices have startup surges?", "How do breakers and circuit limits constrain what can run together?"], "misconceptions": ["High voltage automatically means high energy use.", "Battery capacity alone tells you whether a device will run.", "Nameplate numbers always equal real-world operating behavior."], "formulas_or_rules": ["Power is rate; energy is quantity over time.", "Add running loads before comparing them to available capacity.", "Startup surge and inverter losses reduce effective margin."], "units": ["volt", "amp", "watt", "watt-hour", "kilowatt-hour", "breaker size"]}

{"goal": "Create a ranked list of critical loads, their power draw, duty cycle, and outage priority over different durations.", "deliverables": ["load sheet", "critical load tiers", "device inventory", "usage assumptions"], "acceptance_criteria": ["The audit separates essential, important, and optional loads.", "Each listed load includes at least a reasonable wattage estimate and use pattern.", "The output is specific enough to size backup options."], "time_box_hours": 3.0, "budget_band": "low", "team_size": 1}

{"performance_statement": "Estimate how long a power source can support one or more loads and document the assumptions that change the answer most.", "inputs": ["usable energy estimate", "load sheet", "efficiency assumptions", "duty cycle"], "tools": ["calculator", "worksheet or spreadsheet"], "materials": ["load sheet", "runtime template"], "steps_summary": ["Identify usable energy and convert it into a common unit.", "Select one or more loads and estimate whether they run continuously or intermittently.", "Account for conversion losses or practical margins.", "Calculate runtime for essential scenarios.", "Record the assumptions that most affect the result."], "success_criteria": ["The result uses consistent units throughout.", "The learner can explain how duty cycle and losses change the answer.", "The runtime table is readable by another household member."], "failure_modes": ["Mixing watts and watt-hours.", "Ignoring surge, duty cycle, or efficiency losses.", "Assuming rated capacity is fully usable in all conditions."], "safety_boundary": "This is a planning calculation, not a substitute for manufacturer limits, electrical code, or safe generator and battery practices."}

{"goal": "Create a practical backup power plan with tiered options, tradeoffs, and clear matching to critical loads and outage duration goals.", "deliverables": ["backup power plan", "budget-tier comparison", "decision log", "maintenance checklist"], "acceptance_criteria": ["Each tier names what it can and cannot support.", "The plan documents assumptions, local constraints, and maintenance burdens.", "The decision log explains why one tier is preferred for the current situation."], "time_box_hours": 3.5, "budget_band": "low", "team_size": 1}

{"goal": "Create a local map of people, places, and assets that could support mutual aid, repair, food access, water access, and emergency coordination.", "deliverables": ["local resource map", "contact and capability list", "sharing protocol notes"], "acceptance_criteria": ["The map includes both physical resources and human skills.", "The output distinguishes public, private, and permission-based resources.", "Contact or access notes are clear enough to be useful but respectful of privacy."], "time_box_hours": 3.0, "budget_band": "free", "team_size": 1}

{"goal": "Prepare and deliver a short lesson that transfers one usable capability and leaves behind a handout, checklist, or map that others can reuse.", "deliverables": ["lesson plan", "one-page handout", "micro-session notes", "participant feedback notes"], "acceptance_criteria": ["The lesson has a clear promise, steps, and artifact.", "A general audience can follow the language without insider jargon.", "At least one participant could use the handout without the instructor present."], "time_box_hours": 2.5, "budget_band": "free", "team_size": 1}
